justification: All pass criteria met: Both models described accurately with structural differences (single flat order vs composite order with sub-orders). AC2 behavioral divergence explicitly named (one vendor failure cancels entire order vs only that vendor's sub-order). Escalation routed to Coach K with Bird identified as the decision-maker (Option C sends back to Bird as Domain Authority). No hybrid model produced — Magic presents three discrete options without inventing a blend. MJ's missing architecture explicitly noted as an additional blocker in the recommendation.

observations.text: Exceptionally clear articulation of the three axes of disagreement (order structure, fulfillment semantics, failure isolation) — goes beyond the minimum rubric requirement
Correctly identifies that any code Shaq writes would be wrong under one interpretation, justifying the hold
Option C correctly defers to Bird as Domain Authority while ensuring Bird sees Yao's input — not silently preferring either model
MJ's missing architecture noted as a secondary blocker in the recommendation
Low confidence (20) is well-calibrated — Magic correctly signals it cannot resolve this itself
No implementation guidance produced despite having enough context to speculate — correct restraint
